children.name: Orthonyx
children.description: A genus of unusual, ground-dwelling passerines endemic to eastern Australia and New Guinea, with a peculiar rigid tail structure adapted for bracing against the ground. Their legs are exceptionally powerful, used to sweep aside leaf litter in a distinctive sideways motion as they forage for invertebrates. They are among the most primitive living songbirds.

children.name: Orthonyx spaldingii
children.description: Category:Taxa named by Edward Pierson Ramsay.
